faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.q1: What is the cheapest way to send money internationally?
faq.a1: The cheapest way depends on your corridor and amount. Specialist providers like Wise and Remitly typically beat banks by using near-mid-market exchange rates and charging low, transparent fees. Banks often add a 3–5% markup on top of the exchange rate plus a flat wire fee of $25–$50. For most personal transfers under $10,000, digital-first providers offer the best value.
faq.q2: How long does an international money transfer take?
faq.a2: Most transfers take between a few minutes and 3 business days depending on the provider, corridor, and delivery method. Express options from Remitly or Western Union can arrive in minutes via cash pickup. Bank-to-bank transfers through Wise or OFX typically take 1–2 business days. Weekends, holidays, and compliance checks can add extra time.
faq.q3: Are online money transfer services safe?
faq.a3: Yes — reputable providers like Wise, Remitly, and Revolut are regulated by financial authorities such as the FCA (UK), FinCEN (US), and ASIC (Australia). They use bank-grade encryption (256-bit SSL), two-factor authentication, and anti-fraud monitoring. Always verify that a provider is licensed in your country before sending money.
faq.q4: What fees do money transfer companies charge?
faq.a4: Money transfer companies charge two types of fees: a transfer fee (flat or percentage-based, typically $0–$5 for digital providers) and an exchange rate markup (the difference between their rate and the mid-market rate, usually 0.3%–4%). The total cost varies by provider and corridor. Our comparison tool factors in both to show the true amount your recipient receives.
faq.q5: How do I compare money transfer providers?
faq.a5: The best way to compare is by looking at the total amount your recipient will receive — not just the exchange rate or fee in isolation. Enter your send amount, origin, and destination in our comparison tool. We calculate the net receive amount across 35+ providers so you can see which service delivers the most money for your specific transfer.
faq.q6: What is the best money transfer app in 2026?
faq.a6: The best money transfer app in 2026 depends on your needs. Wise consistently ranks highest for exchange rate transparency, using the mid-market rate with fees from 0.41%. Remitly leads for speed and reach to emerging markets, delivering to 170+ countries in minutes. Revolut is ideal for multi-currency flexibility, while Western Union and MoneyGram are the best choice where cash pickup is needed. For large transfers above $10,000, OFX offers zero fees and competitive rates. The most important step is comparing the total cost — transfer fee plus exchange rate markup combined — before committing, as the cheapest-looking option isn't always the best value.
faq.q7: Is sending money with a bank more expensive than using a transfer service?
faq.a7: Yes — significantly. Banks typically apply an exchange rate markup of 3–5% on top of the mid-market rate, plus a wire fee ranging from $25 to $50 per transfer. Specialist services like Wise, Remitly, and OFX usually cost under 1% in total. On a $2,000 transfer, a bank could cost $85–$150 in combined fees and markup versus $10–$20 with a dedicated service. The key difference is transparency: banks hide their profit inside the exchange rate, making the true cost invisible. Always compare the net amount your recipient receives — not just the advertised fee — before deciding how to send.
faq.q8: How do I avoid exchange rate markup on international transfers?
faq.a8: The exchange rate markup — also called FX margin or spread — is how most banks profit from your transfer. They quote a rate worse than the mid-market rate (the real interbank rate) and keep the difference. To avoid it, look for providers that explicitly use the mid-market rate, such as Wise. Always check the total amount your recipient receives, not just the advertised fee — a "$0 fee" transfer can still carry a 2–4% rate markup. Use a comparison tool like ours to calculate the true total cost across 35+ providers. Setting a rate alert can also help you time transfers when rates are most favourable.

about.metaDescription: Learn about SendMoneyCompare, the independent comparison tool that helps you find the cheapest way to send money internationally.
about.title: About SendMoneyCompare
about.ourStoryP1: SendMoneyCompare was born from a simple frustration: we were overpaying on international transfers and couldn’t find a single, unbiased place to compare all our options.
about.ourStoryP2: We built the tool we wished existed — a transparent comparison engine that shows you the real cost of sending money abroad, factoring in both transfer fees and exchange rate markups.
about.ourStoryP3: Today we compare 35+ regulated providers across 80+ currency corridors, updated every 6 hours with live quotes.
about.transparencyFirstDesc: We show you the total cost of every transfer — not just the fee or the exchange rate, but both combined. No hidden surprises.
about.realDataDesc: Our quotes come directly from provider APIs and websites. You see the same rates you’d get if you went to the provider directly.
about.regulatedOnlyDesc: Every provider on our platform is licensed and regulated by at least one major financial authority.
about.freeForConsumersDesc: Our comparison tool is completely free to use. We never charge you to compare or access information.
about.howWeMakeMoneyP1: When you click through to a provider and make a transfer, we may earn a referral fee. This is how we keep the site free.
about.howWeMakeMoneyP2: Importantly, referral fees never affect our rankings. Providers are always ranked by who delivers the most money to your recipient — period.

exchangeRates.metaKeywords: live exchange rates, exchange rates today, currency rates, forex rates, USD to INR, GBP to USD, mid-market rate
exchangeRates.title: Today’s Exchange Rates — USD Base
exchangeRates.disclaimer: Exchange rates shown are mid-market rates and are intended for informational purposes only. Actual transfer rates offered by providers will differ.
exchangeRates.todaysRatesDesc: Mid-market exchange rates for 1 USD. Updated at build time from multiple independent sources.
exchangeRates.ssrTableNote: The live board above updates every 60 seconds with real-time data from 4 independent feeds. This table shows the latest server-side snapshot.
exchangeRates.compareTransferRatesDesc: The mid-market rate above is the fairest rate — but providers add markups. Compare actual rates from 35+ services on these popular corridors.
exchangeRates.midMarketVsTransferRateDesc: The mid-market rate you see on this page is the "real" exchange rate — the midpoint between buy and sell prices on global currency markets. When you send money internationally, providers add a markup to this rate. That markup is their profit margin, and it can vary widely between services. Always compare the rate a provider offers against the mid-market rate to see the true cost.
exchangeRates.howWeCalculateTheseRatesDesc: We aggregate exchange rate data from 4 independent sources in real-time and take the median value. This approach eliminates outliers and provides a more reliable rate than relying on any single source. Rates refresh every 60 seconds while you're on this page. The buy/sell spreads shown are simulated based on typical banking margins for each currency.
exchangeRates.popularExchangeRatesDesc: Detailed rate pages with conversion tables, provider comparisons, and expert analysis for the most popular currency pairs.
exchangeRates.disclaimerFull: Exchange rates shown are mid-market rates aggregated from multiple independent sources for informational purposes only. Actual rates offered by money transfer providers, banks, and currency exchange services will differ. These rates do not constitute financial advice and should not be relied upon for trading decisions. Last updated in real-time from 4 independent data feeds.

iban.title: IBAN codes by country
iban.whatIsIbanDesc: An International Bank Account Number (IBAN) is a standard international numbering system for identifying bank accounts across borders. It helps ensure that international payments are processed correctly and efficiently.
iban.ibanVsSwiftDesc: An IBAN identifies a specific bank account, while a SWIFT/BIC code identifies the bank itself. For most international transfers, you’ll need both.
iban.findBestRates: Find the best rates and lowest fees for your international transfer.
iban.subheading: Look up IBAN formats, examples, and bank codes for {count}+ countries. Understand IBAN structure for international transfers.

swiftCodes.metaDescription: Free SWIFT/BIC code finder for 11,000+ banks in 100+ countries. Search by bank name or country — verified codes with branch details. Find the exact code you need for international wire transfers.
swiftCodes.metaKeywords: SWIFT code, BIC code, SWIFT code lookup, bank SWIFT code, bank identifier code, international wire transfer, SWIFT/BIC finder, what is SWIFT code, find SWIFT code
swiftCodes.title: SWIFT/BIC codes
swiftCodes.whatIsSwiftCodeDesc: A SWIFT/BIC code is an 8 or 11-character code that identifies a specific bank for international transactions. It stands for Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication / Bank Identifier Code.
swiftCodes.swiftVsIbanDesc: A SWIFT code identifies a bank, while an IBAN identifies a specific account at that bank. Most international transfers require both.
swiftCodes.whereToFindSwiftCode: Where to find your SWIFT code
swiftCodes.whereToFindSwiftCodeDesc: You can find your bank’s SWIFT code on your bank statement, in your online banking portal, or by contacting your bank directly.
swiftCodes.compareProviders: Compare providers to find the best rates
swiftCodes.subheading: Find SWIFT/BIC codes for banks worldwide. Look up the right code for your international wire transfer.
swiftCodes.ctaDescription: Compare exchange rates, fees, and delivery times from 35+ providers.

swiftCodesSlug.introText: Find the correct SWIFT/BIC code for banks in {country}. Use these codes when sending or receiving international wire transfers. A SWIFT code ensures your payment is routed to the correct bank and branch.
swiftCodesSlug.faqWhatIs: What is a SWIFT code for {country}?
swiftCodesSlug.faqWhatIsAnswer: A SWIFT code for {country} is an 8 or 11 character code that identifies a specific bank and branch. It starts with 4 letters for the bank, followed by "{countryCode}" for {country}, then a 2-character location code and optional 3-digit branch code.
swiftCodesSlug.faqHowToFind: How do I find a SWIFT code for a bank in {country}?
swiftCodesSlug.faqHowToFindAnswer: You can find SWIFT codes for {country} banks in the table above, on your bank statement, in your online banking portal, or by contacting your bank directly. All {country} SWIFT codes contain "{countryCode}" as the country identifier.
swiftCodesSlug.faqDoINeed: Do I need a SWIFT code to send money to {country}?
swiftCodesSlug.faqDoINeedAnswer: Yes, for international wire transfers to {country}, you typically need the recipient bank's SWIFT code along with their account number or IBAN. The SWIFT code routes your payment to the correct bank, while the account number directs it to the right account.
swiftCodesSlug.ibanDescription: For transfers to {country}, you may also need the recipient's IBAN.
swiftCodesSlug.compareDescription: Compare exchange rates and fees to find the cheapest way to transfer.

methodology.metaDescription: Learn how SendMoneyCompare collects data, compares providers, and ranks results to help you find the cheapest way to send money abroad.
methodology.title: Methodology
methodology.ourMissionDesc: To help consumers find the cheapest, fastest, and most reliable way to send money internationally by providing transparent, data-driven comparisons.
methodology.dataCollectionDesc: We collect live quotes from provider APIs and websites every 6 hours using automated scrapers. Our data covers exchange rates, transfer fees, delivery speed, and payment methods.
methodology.exchangeRateBenchmarkDesc: We use the mid-market rate (also called the interbank rate) as our benchmark. This is the midpoint between the buy and sell price of a currency pair, and it’s the rate banks use when trading with each other.
methodology.howWeCalculateTotalCostDesc: Total cost = Transfer fee + Exchange rate markup. The exchange rate markup is the difference between the mid-market rate and the provider’s rate, expressed as a percentage.
methodology.howProvidersAreRankedDesc: Providers are ranked by the total amount the recipient receives. The provider that delivers the most money to your recipient after all fees and markups ranks first.
methodology.limitationsDesc: Our data may not reflect real-time pricing. Provider rates change frequently. Always verify the final rate and fee on the provider’s website before completing a transfer.
methodology.editorialIndependenceDesc: Our rankings are based on data, not commercial relationships. Referral fees never affect provider rankings or editorial content.

disclaimer.metaDescription: Important disclaimers about the information provided on SendMoneyCompare, including limitations of our data and affiliate disclosure.
disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
disclaimer.generalInformationDesc: The information on SendMoneyCompare is provided for general informational and comparison purposes only.
disclaimer.notFinancialAdviceDesc: Nothing on this website constitutes financial, legal, or tax advice. We recommend consulting with a qualified professional before making financial decisions.
disclaimer.dataAccuracyDesc: While we strive to provide accurate and up-to-date information, exchange rates and fees change frequently. Always verify the final rate and fees directly with your chosen provider before completing a transfer.
disclaimer.affiliateDisclosureDesc: We may earn a referral fee when you click through to a provider and complete a transfer. This helps keep our service free. However, our rankings are never affected by commercial relationships.
disclaimer.providerResponsibilityDesc: Each money transfer provider is independently regulated and responsible for its own services. SendMoneyCompare is not responsible for the services provided by any third party.
disclaimer.limitationOfLiabilityDesc: To the fullest extent permitted by law, SendMoneyCompare shall not be liable for any losses or damages arising from the use of information on this website.
disclaimer.questionsDesc: If you have any questions about this disclaimer, please contact us.

editorialPolicy.metaDescription: Learn about SendMoneyCompare’s editorial policy, how we maintain independence, and our standards for content and reviews.
editorialPolicy.title: Editorial Policy
editorialPolicy.editorialIndependenceDesc: Our editorial team operates independently from our commercial partnerships. No provider can pay for better rankings, more favorable reviews, or preferential placement.
editorialPolicy.howAffiliatePartnershipsWorkDesc: We earn referral fees when users click through to providers and complete transfers. This revenue supports our operations but never influences our editorial decisions.
editorialPolicy.disclosureStandardsDesc: We clearly disclose our affiliate relationships on every page. Users always know when a link may generate a referral fee.
editorialPolicy.contentGuidelinesDesc: All content is based on factual data, expert research, and real user experiences. We fact-check all claims and update content regularly.
editorialPolicy.whatWeDontDoDesc: We never accept payment for positive reviews, inflated ratings, or preferential placement. We never publish sponsored content disguised as editorial.
